title: What NGE can teach us about
display_title: What NGE can teach us about
wikitext:
[]: What NGE can teach us about...             Quote Post by soda7777777 » Mon Aug 30, 2010 2:37 am Human nature. So I've felt after watching enough of the series, that you can definitely learn about yourself as well as human instinct and nature. Though your average person may just consider it another mecha anime, with whiny teenagers, it's something far greater. That aspect of the show was merely a vehicle for the story. In reality the show is much more about psychology than it is about giant robots. What I've drawn from the show is this. First, no matter the setting, humans are bound to have the same behaviors. Just look at how the characters treat life as normal. I mean it's not consistent. But sometimes it seems the characters just wanna get away from the world by acting like everything is fine, and that they're living in a world that is pre Second Impact. That's all I'll say for what I've drawn, at least at the moment. I'm more interested in what everyone else has drawn from the show. I'd also like the say that I take the show on an allegorical basis, and that for me the show shows us humans struggling with finding their purpose.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Aug 30, 2010 2:44 am I think I said all I could say on this subject HERE.
